sourceMovie.title: Eden
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Eden (2025) stuck because it commits to its thriller premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Ron Howard's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The thriller and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Dream House
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Eden clicked for you, Dream House (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Client
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Eden clicked for you, The Client (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Player
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Eden clicked for you, The Player (1992) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Eden?
faq.answer: Try Dream House, The Client, The Player—each shares DNA with Eden while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Eden?
faq.answer: Eden (2025) is best described as Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Eden worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
